title: Deeply Savory Mexican Cabbage Barbacoa
description: A hearty, winey, and snappy Veracruz-style Mexican dish featuring tender white cabbage, rich beef, and a depth of flavor provided by chipotle peppers and oregano.
instructions: Cut the chuck roast into large chunks and season with salt and black pepper.
Heat 1 tbsp of oil in a large Dutch oven over medium-high heat and sear the beef until browned on all sides.
Remove the beef from the pot and set it aside.
Add the remaining 1 tbsp of oil to the pot and sauté the chipotle peppers until fragrant.
Add the garlic and sauté for another minute.
Add the oregano, lime juice, and 2 cups of water to the pot and bring to a simmer.
Return the beef to the pot and bring to a boil.
Cover the pot and transfer it to the oven at 300°F (150°C) for 2.5 hours, or until the beef is tender.
Shred the beef with two forks and return it to the pot.
Add the shredded cabbage to the pot and cook until it is tender and slightly caramelized.
Season the barbacoa with salt and black pepper to taste.
Serve hot and garnish with chopped fresh cilantro, if desired.
